rules.rule: Kernel critical-state changes must enter through Command handling and preserve the event-before-derived-state transaction boundary. Do not approve direct writes that bypass KernelStore.execute_command for authoritative state.
Hermes/runtime/dashboard/provider surfaces are adapters or projections. Do not let runtime compatibility code become authoritative for gates, spend, model routing, side effects, or operator identity.

Self-improvement changes may produce proposals, evals, patch-review packets, and operator-gated decisions, but must not autonomously mutate active behavior, update frontier routes, or re-execute side effects.

New service-layer code should reduce repeated orchestration or context load while keeping policy and persistence invariants in the kernel/store layer. Avoid broad manager modules and duplicate validation logic.
Event payloads and durable artifacts must not contain raw secrets, credentials, private keys, or unnecessary client data. Use governed artifact references or secret references instead.
